medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient 1 received treatment beginning at 1 month of age. She had two episodes of acute illness followed by seizure before reaching 1 year of age. Although there was no acute deterioration of motor ability after these episodes, her development was slightly delayed. Seizure-like movement had been observed at the age of 10 months after vaccination, and the patient was then prescribed phenobarbital which was later discontinued at 5 years of age. Currently, she exhibits no abnormal neurological signs.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Glutaric aciduria type I (GA-I), treated with special diets, carnitine supplements, and immediate stress avoidance.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Brain MRI T2-weighted images revealing high signal intensity over the globus pallidus were observed at 1.3 years. The pallidal lesions resolved gradually, and disappeared after 6 years. High intensity over deep white matter was more prominent at older ages.
